content.full_text:
Memorandum

To: Gina, Jay, John C., Nan, Helen and Barbara  
Date: Aug. 3, 1978  
File: 82015, 82036, 84210, 84214

From: FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION  
Lee C. Rosenthal

Subject: Returned Contributions

The Commission at its August meeting adopted the following policy concerning the reporting of contributions which are returned to the contributor.

A check which is not a late contribution may be returned to the contributor and not reported if:

(1) The check is not cashed; and

(2) The check is returned prior to the closing date for the campaign statement covering the period during which the check was received.

A check which is a late contribution (i.e., $1,000 or more received after the closing date for the last preelection statement) may be returned and not reported either as a late contribution or on the post election statement if:

(1) The check is not cashed; and

(2) The check is returned within 48 hours of receipt.

Recipients who are given advice concerning return of late contributions should be told that it would be wise to inform the filing officers of the return. If this is not done, the filing officer may send noncompliance notices to the recipient because the contributor may have filed a late contribution report.

Our previous advice did not deal specifically with the late contribution situation and had a further requirement that if a contribution had been solicited by the committee or candidate it could not be returned.

LCR:plh
